ROADMAP 5.3: document figurine fonts for answers section

Font analysis of HastingsFigurine/ZurichFigurine/LinaresFigurine:
- All use K/Q/R/B/N/P (upper=white, lower=black), standard ASCII, no PUA

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Export As PDF with async job manager and PDF caching

Export EPUB books to PDF via context menu in grid and table views.
Chapters are rendered sequentially through a hidden WKWebView, merged
into a single PDFDocument with a navigable outline built from the
EPUB's TOC. Exports run asynchronously via ExportJobManager with
per-job progress tracking, shown in a popover from the library
toolbar. On first export the PDF is cached beside the EPUB; subsequent
exports reuse the cached file. Uses the user's configured font and
adds proper page break CSS between chapters.

commit.message:
fix(e2e): lowercase mock org role + disable SMTP SSL for MailHog

Two fixes:

1. Mock org role 'Owner' → 'owner': Zod's RoleSchema uses lowercase
   enum values. The PascalCase mock role caused MyOrgsResponseSchema
   parse to fail silently, making hasOrgs=false and showing the
   "no organization" empty state on contacts/properties pages.

2. Add ORGSAPI_Smtp__EnableSsl=false to CI .env: MailHog is a
   plain-text SMTP server. SmtpEmailSender defaults EnableSsl=true
   when the config key is missing, causing TLS handshake failure
   on invitation emails (HTTP 500 in invite-member test).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor(result): split repositories, save-on-done flow, and UI fixes

- Split ImageRepository into HistoryRepository (CRUD) and
  ImageProcessingRepository (ML processing only); processing no
  longer writes to Hive during the pipeline
- Add SaveRecordUseCase: history is saved only when the user taps
  Done (face) or Open PDF (document), back/discard skips saving
- Document result shows a single "Open PDF" button that saves then
  opens the file; face result keeps a "Done" button
- Remove all debug code from DocumentProcessingService (_debugCorners,
  debugPrint calls, /tmp/ file writes)
- Rename GradientThumbnail → HistoryThumbnail; add OcrTextPanel
  (extracted-text viewer with search + copy)
- Fix history-detail overflow: portrait thumbnail max height 180→140 px
- HomeBinding trimmed to history-only services; ProcessingBinding owns
  all ML service registrations

commit.message:
fix: wrap PDF generation in ob_start to capture stray DomPDF output

Root cause: DomPDF/blade rendering produces stray output (warnings, notices)
that gets prepended to the JSON response body, making it unparseable by
axios (response.data becomes a string instead of parsed JSON object).
Fix: ob_start() before generation, ob_get_clean() after, log any stray
output for diagnosis. Version bumped to 2026-03-08c.

commit.message:
Add strict verify-then-store protocol to eliminate false positives

Rule #2b rewritten with concrete verification steps for each vuln type:
- SQLi: must show data leak, not just 200 response
- XSS: must be unescaped in executable HTML context
- NoSQL: check tech stack first — if SQL DB, skip NoSQLi entirely
- SSRF: response must contain actual internal content
- CSRF: must prove state change happened
- CMDi: response must contain command output
- SSTI: verify with two different computations
- Path traversal: response must contain actual file content
- Open redirect: parse URL hostname properly

Also added deduplication: same vuln+endpoint stored once, not 3 times.
Each finding gets affected_endpoints list for all confirmed paths.

game: Frame Arc propagated — Infinite Game entity, imposition→excavation, test tokens

- Entity: Computer Future → Infinite Game throughout UI + system prompt

- Phase labels: CLI = enrollment/imposition, chat = excavation mode
- System prompt: Frame Arc embedded with phase-specific guidance
- Andy → my boss in all user-facing strings
- Test tokens: POST /admin/token generates 24hr tokens, GET /?t= validates + embeds
- /api/start: validates test_token, sets is_test=1, marks token used
- /api/sync: pings cf-internal Discord with session summary
- Test endgame: leaderboard gating note, sync/real-run choice, Discord ping
- test_tokens table migrated on staging + prod

feature: class-based children management system

Restructures the data model so children belong to a class, eliminating
repetitive per-child entry of teacher, school, and grade. Field workers
create a class once (with school, grade, teacher, home language), then
add children to it.

- DB: adds schools (admin-managed) and classes tables with full RLS;
  drops teacher/class/school columns from children, adds class_id + gender
- Storage: SCHOOLS and CLASSES keys, full offline-first CRUD for classes
- Sync: CLASSES added to pipeline between GROUPS and CHILDREN (FK order)
- ClassesContext: separate context with cache-first loading, prevSyncingRef
  pattern, and school fetch fallback for stale-isOnline race condition
- UI: ChildrenListScreen reworked to class-card view; new CreateClass,
  EditClass, ClassDetailScreen; AddChild/EditChild simplified to 4 fields;
  ChildSelector shows class name; Manage Groups moved inside class detail
- All dropdowns use Portal/Dialog/RadioButton pattern (fixes ScrollView bug)
- Tests: Jest + jest-expo configured; 22 tests across storage, context, UI

commit.message:
feat: add IHME GBD burden data pipeline with DALYs and Deaths support

Add pipeline to load IHME Global Burden of Disease data for research
intensity analysis in Lens C. Supports multiple CSV downloads (one per
metric: Number, Percent, Rate) and stores both DALYs and Deaths as
burden measures, enabling sensitivity analysis.

- pipeline/07_gbd_burden.py: auto-detects and merges all CSVs in data/gbd/
- data/taxonomy/topic_burden_map.csv: 22-row mapping of 8 topic categories
  to GBD Level 2 causes
- docs/gbd_burden_methodology.md: documents Level 2 rationale, excluded
  topics, DALYs vs Deaths justification, and schema design
- pipeline/00_setup_db.py: schema updated with separate measure/metric columns
- notebooks/lens_C_topic_trends.ipynb: updated queries for new schema,
  added Deaths-based sensitivity comparison chart in C1 and GBD overlay
  in C4 NCD transition analysis
- .gitignore: excludes data/gbd/ (raw IHME downloads)

commit.message:
feat: redesign dashboard with TUI/Ocean Depths theme

- Replace Bootstrap card layout with a custom TUI aesthetic using
  Ocean Depths palette, JetBrains Mono, and a 2×2 CSS grid
- Add AlertsCard (health checks for CPU/mem/disk/temp/voltage) and
  StatusRibbon (live stats bar pinned below the navbar)
- Extend models: VoltageMetrics, SwapTotalKB/SwapUsedKB/CachedKB on
  MemoryMetrics, OsDescription/KernelVersion/Architecture on SystemInfo
- Collect voltage via vcgencmd + rpi_volt hwmon; cache hwmon path on
  first call to avoid repeated directory scans each poll cycle
- Extract shared TuiStyle helpers (BarClass/TextColor/DotColor with
  parameterised thresholds) to eliminate 12 duplicate one-liners across
  cards and the status ribbon
- Fix CPU alert threshold (85 → 90) to match CpuCard display
- Fix Available% in MemoryCard: was hardcoded green, now colour-coded
  via UsagePercent like the rest of the card
- Remove TOCTOU File.Exists guards in the process and hwmon collectors
- Make poll interval configurable via MetricsIntervalSeconds in appsettings

commit.message:
Add gradual star scaling to prevent infinite prestige farming

Each owned star raises the threshold by 0.02 in log10 (~5% more per star).
Early game unchanged (0 stars: 1e10 for first star). At 100 stars you
get ~4-5 per run, at 300 ~2, ceiling around 400 stars. Prevents rapid
farming while keeping meaningful long-term progression.

commit.message:
feat: inject store context into AI system prompt

POST /ai/chat now loads settings:{tenantId} and admin:menu:list:{tenantId}
from KV and injects store name, address, business hours, closed days,
booking URL, cancellation policy, and menu summary into the system prompt.

This transforms the AI from a generic assistant into one that can answer
"営業時間は？" "どこにありますか？" "メニューは？" with real store data.

Prompt rules updated to reference store context for accurate answers.
Missing fields are safely omitted. Menu capped at 10 items.
